277213.explanation: If there are budget deficits, you should not only cut costs but also increase the property tax (OZB). That is fairer than placing the burden on welfare organizations and residents who need support, such as in youth care or poverty reduction. Also, the OZB in Soest is low compared to other municipalities.

277214.explanation: Status holders are also residents of Soest and have rights to priority like other urgent seekers. They are not THE cause of the housing shortage. There is only one cause: the failing housing policy of recent years. Too little social housing has been built recently.

277215.explanation: It is a cheap way to keep warm and saves costs. At the same time, it can cause health problems and is bad for the environment. We want a ban on burning when the 'burning guide' gives negative advice due to unfavorable weather and air quality.

277216.explanation: The Youth Fund for sports and culture is an excellent resource for children (and adults) who have financial difficulties to participate in sports and/or music education. This is good for children's physical, social, and mental development and prevents exclusion!

277217.explanation: The municipality has the ability to deploy enforcement officers to improve residents' sense of safety where the police have no capacity.

277218.explanation: Using drugs (including soft drugs) is bad for health. We must therefore reduce stimuli especially for young people. We even advocate for a nicotine-free generation. A coffee shop where soft drugs are sold and smoked certainly does not fit in with that!

277219.explanation: This makes it easy for residents to contact the police for minor questions, neighborhood nuisances, safety advice, and reporting suspicious situations. The neighborhood officer becomes known and visible to the people, which builds more trust.

277220.explanation: Increasing the starter's loan offers first-time buyers more financial room to purchase their first home. The biggest advantage is that the loan is interest and repayment-free for the first three years, resulting in lower monthly costs, so it is a great tool especially for young people!

277221.explanation: We want to give people who live in Soest and Soesterberg and want to stay here as much chance as possible to do so. We want to focus on giving maximum allocation priority to current residents of our municipality.

277222.explanation: There is a great need for homes, especially social rental. Soest currently has a significant shortage! If in the coming years we build 40% of new homes as social rental, we can better meet the actual demand for social rental homes in Soest and Soesterberg.

277223.explanation: Space for housing is scarce. Especially in our municipality where we live in a fantastic natural environment that we also cherish. It is therefore good to make better use of space by building more stories where appropriate without harming the village character.

277224.explanation: When transformation is not financially feasible or too risky, the municipality should have the option to financially support developers. It is important that clear agreements are made about the distribution of any profits.

277228.explanation: Traffic safety and livability are our top priorities. If 50 km/h roads cannot be made safe, we believe the speed limit should be lowered. The chance of accidents is greatly reduced at lower speeds with more room for slow traffic (pedestrians and cyclists).

277229.explanation: We want to introduce a Citizens' Assembly in Soest, where residents think together about important topics. Residents can suggest topics themselves, such as waste or the location for a new theater. This shows that we take residents seriously, give trust, and make better choices together.

277231.explanation: This alderman can delve deeper into and gain insight into local issues of Soesterberg. It offers benefits such as more involvement, direct communication between Soesterberg residents and the municipal government, so that solutions for local issues come faster.

277232.explanation: It can be good for transparency, but in a small municipality like Soest it is quite manageable. This information can be requested. We wonder if this is necessary given the additional costs involved.

277233.explanation: We want to prevent vacancies and strengthen shopping centers by mainly allowing retail in existing centers. We also see shopping centers as meeting places for residents. We want to appoint a shopping street manager to get empty shops filled again with local and original stores.

277234.explanation: Where sustainability lags behind, the municipality takes proactive leadership. Also to ensure the energy transition happens fairly: regardless of the size of your wallet, you should be able to participate, even as an entrepreneur!

277236.explanation: Separation has more advantages. For example, collected plastic and paper are more reusable. Source separation is also much better for the environment and people.

277238.explanation: The municipality can support initiatives from farmers by acting as a facilitator, but not directly financially.

277239.explanation: A beautifully naturally designed environment that invites people to experience it is good for residents. We encourage people to swap tiles for greenery in their gardens, also in public spaces. Of course, this also has many benefits for animals and plants.

277241.explanation: We are in favor of space for music and creativity. A beautiful bandstand also suits the (future) Van Weedeplein, which will be good for meetings and enjoyment for young and old.

277242.explanation: Children feel more comfortable on a green schoolyard. They are more challenged there and play more than on a 'gray' playground. This is not only good for health but also leads to better learning performances.

277243.explanation: We want sports to be possible for everyone. Young or old - beginner or advanced. We do this not only by supporting our clubs but also by designing public spaces that invite physical activity. Think of attractive walking and cycling routes and play areas.

277245.explanation: As GroenLinks-PvdA we want to invest in a safe living environment: in clean neighborhoods with good street lighting! The feeling of safety is just as important.

277247.explanation: However, this question is premature for now. Building behind the Schans is a logical location to investigate for housing after both sides of the Stadhouderslaan (as currently planned) have been developed or discarded as housing locations.

277248.explanation: This preserves important social functions because they save considerably on rent and are not dependent on market parties. The municipality ensures they remain accessible to everyone.

277249.explanation: We support all actions that tackle the housing shortage. If temporary housing helps with that, for example by using future housing locations earlier for temporary homes, it would be a win-win situation where possible.

277250.explanation: We will transform the second part of Van Weedestraat (where the market is) into Van Weedeplein. Together we will make it a lively and vibrant center. It will be accessible to cyclists but not mopeds and scooters. Suppliers will retain access at certain times.

277251.explanation: This is already mandatory! People on welfare are legally required to actively seek work and accept offered work. Otherwise, the municipality can reduce or terminate the benefit. These rules apply to everyone up to retirement age, except those with a disability.

277252.explanation: In recent years we have invested heavily in fast cycling routes. By encouraging cycling, we make our municipality more livable and greener. Think of safe bike paths to schools, sports locations, workplaces, and continuous cycling routes where everyone can ride comfortably and safely.

277253.explanation: Faster green lights for cyclists ensure smoother traffic flow, less waiting time, and more comfort, making cycling a more attractive alternative to the car. It is healthy and encourages more physical activity.

277254.explanation: A lively shopping center also requires good parking facilities, especially if we want a low-car shopping area (with a Van Weedeplein) around part of Van Weedestraat.

277255.explanation: On average, a caregiver provides 13 hours of care per week. We must take good care of these people. More and more caregivers feel overburdened. Increased support for caregivers helps them continue to care for their loved ones.

277256.explanation: That is much clearer than having to apply repeatedly for a provision. It is recognizable for everyone, just like, for example, the U-pass is well known in Utrecht.

277257.explanation: Prevention is better than cure. Providing information about money, borrowing, and debts can prevent problems or directly address existing financial issues before they become bigger. This saves society money in the long term.

277258.explanation: Meeting places are spots where people meet, where friendships arise, where young and old find each other, and where neighbors collaborate and inspire one another.

277259.explanation: Cycling is healthy, causes no noise or emissions nuisance, and takes up less space. Fast cycling routes help those who can ride faster by literally removing the hurdles.

277260.explanation: We favor as few rules and hoops as possible. Smaller events should be encouraged as much as possible. This creates a lively village with activities for young and old.

277261.explanation: The municipality should reserve money per neighborhood for residents' plans. The residents of the neighborhood choose which plan is implemented.

277262.explanation: With Amersfoort already placing windmills just across the border, this is a very suitable location. This can be the start of an energy-neutral Soest. Which can also generate revenue for Soest society.

277263.explanation: We are taking steps to create one welfare organization instead of multiple ones to better support residents. This means SWOS and Balans will merge. So one desk and one point of contact.

277264.explanation: Soesterberg has contributed for years with the shelter location just across the municipal border in Zeist. Now it is time for Soest core to find suitable locations within its village boundaries.

277265.explanation: Sex workers have a higher risk of labor exploitation. When they, or others facing exploitation, want to escape this situation, the municipality must support them.

277267.explanation: More municipal efforts on work for status holders have many advantages. It accelerates integration, reduces dependency on benefits, thereby decreasing pressure on social assistance. It also promotes economic independence and helps fill vacancies.

277268.explanation: In some situations, it is strategically better to purchase land so you can build what is needed rather than what is most profitable, and as a municipality, you also have control over speeding up housing construction.

277213.explanation: Soest2002 keeps costs affordable and does not want additional tax increases.

277214.explanation: We stand for shelter within legal frameworks; without priority over other urgent housing seekers.

277215.explanation: We consider wood burning and its effects on health and air quality important. No total ban, but a burning signal ('code red') on days with poor air quality, based on responsibility, preferably regulated nationally and widely supported in Soest.

277216.explanation: Everyone should be able to participate; also in sports and therefore with support to clubs.

277217.explanation: More enforcement officers (BOA's) are not necessary. Our villages remain safe with increased supervision, during day and evening. However, BOA's must be more visible.

277219.explanation: Attention to safety and reports is needed, but weekly neighborhood police consultation hours are not necessary.

277220.explanation: Due to the sharp rise in house prices, €30,000 is insufficient to truly help starters buy their first home. A higher starter loan increases opportunities for young residents and families to find affordable housing in Soest—and it costs the community no money.

277221.explanation: Priority for first-time homebuyers would be good.

277222.explanation: The existing target of 30% social rental housing ensures a sufficiently balanced composition in our neighborhoods.

277223.explanation: To maintain the village character we desire, we are cautious about adding high-rise buildings.

277224.explanation: No subsidies but support and fast procedures.

277228.explanation: Main roads should remain 50 km/h roads to ensure sufficient traffic flow.

277229.explanation: We support the idea of participation. A randomly selected group of residents can sometimes be a supplement, but it is not a standard solution for us. We choose participation that is widely supported and allows space for all voices in society.

277231.explanation: Although Soesterberg receives attention and feels represented by the Council and Executive Board, this could be a good addition.

277232.explanation: This is impossible due to the large number of often spontaneous contacts. Substantive contacts are always recorded.

277233.explanation: From the perspective of livability and strengthening shopping centers, we are actually in favor of concentrating shops.

277234.explanation: No subsidies but support and fast procedures.

277236.explanation: Paying for residual waste often leads to dumping in rural areas. For Soest and Soesterberg, this is too big a risk.

277238.explanation: Financial support is a matter for the national and provincial governments.

277239.explanation: Our municipality is already doing well here: this must continue with extra attention for disadvantages in certain areas and reports must continue to be properly followed up.

277241.explanation: We are strong supporters of local arts and cultural events. At the permanent but also temporary locations that already exist. Temporary stages are more flexible and efficient than a fixed bandstand in one place.

277242.explanation: Schools decide for themselves what they want to spend on plants and trees. Moreover, this is often realized through sponsorship.

277243.explanation: A livable and green environment also requires fields where people can meet and sports can be played.

277245.explanation: There are still places in Soest where lighting is absent or poor. This must improve because it is safer.

277247.explanation: We need more housing. Behind de Schans is an excellent location along the edge of the village to build a residential neighborhood.

277248.explanation: The need is there and our municipality now has enough money to invest in this.

277249.explanation: It is not the municipality’s role to place housing. This remains the task of private individuals, developers, and housing corporations.

277250.explanation: In the redesign of Van Weedestraat (over 10 years ago) this was already considered with the shopkeepers and was not regarded as positive.

277251.explanation: Of course, this does not apply to people who cannot work!

277252.explanation: This is a task of the province. The municipality should certainly continue to advocate this.

277253.explanation: Whenever traffic lights are renewed, extra attention must be given to this.

277254.explanation: Especially in the residential neighborhoods around shopping centers, this should be focused on.

277255.explanation: Of course, the municipality must support informal caregivers. And if demand for support increases, this can require more funding.

277256.explanation: This is a good method to support existing funds, such as youth sports and culture and Stichting Leergeld.

277257.explanation: Not only providing information via 'Geld op Orde' in Soest (GOOS), but also continuing with debt counseling.

277258.explanation: Where possible, connect with existing associations or other initiatives.

277259.explanation: This is mostly a task of the province. Of course, the municipality should continue to pay attention to and advocate for 'fast and safe' cycling.

277260.explanation: More trust in organizers and fewer detailed regulations. From a 'yes, provided' attitude, with safety remaining paramount.

277261.explanation: Existing arrangements must be applied and better communicated.

277262.explanation: No wind turbines in or near our residential neighborhoods.

277263.explanation: Soest can be proud of organizations like SWOS and Balans that do beautiful and good work focused on their own target groups.

277264.explanation: Soesterberg has done its part in recent years. If it has to happen again, then not in Soesterberg.

277265.explanation: Not only sex workers but all job seekers should receive support in finding work.

277267.explanation: All job seekers – including status holders – should receive support in finding work.

277268.explanation: All means should be explored to enable faster housing construction.

277213.explanation: We want to keep the costs for our residents as low as possible. We will increase municipal charges by no more than inflation. Property tax may not increase more than the general price development. By the way, the dog tax should be abolished.

277214.explanation: The municipality complies with the Distribution Act and this will remain in place for the time being. Soest must take its role in accommodating refugees and other people in need in small reception locations. According to the new coalition, municipalities should continue to decide for themselves whether to give status holders priority for a social rental home. We support plans to solve the underlying problems (including less inflow), making giving priority no longer legally possible in the long term.

277215.explanation: Wood burning causes not only odor but also unhealthy fine particles in the air. The municipality should actively promote the Burning Guide and encourage residents to engage in conversation. By talking with residents, we make it discussable whether a ban is necessary.

277216.explanation: GGS continues to commit to affordable sports and supports existing minimum schemes to which residents with an income up to 130% of the social assistance standard are entitled. We ensure that these arrangements remain accessible to residents who are financially struggling.

277217.explanation: If necessary, we will deploy more enforcement officers (BOAs) in public spaces. Safety for everyone is our approach. It is important that sufficient resources are available so the municipality can actively address unnecessary nuisance at its source, the offender, and enforce more strictly.

277218.explanation: There is national legislation that coffeeshops must comply with. Residents should be able to choose for themselves whether to use the supply and decide if it contributes to their own health and a pleasant life.

277219.explanation: The neighborhood police officer must be more accessible. A fixed and visible contact moment weekly, or at least monthly, in the community center, supermarket, or neighborhood center is very important. We also support better digital visibility of the neighborhood police officer.

277220.explanation: Sounds strange, but this amount is in addition to the regular mortgage. Increasing the starter’s loan thus increases the housing costs for the buyer. However, we believe that more volume, that is, more funds, should be made available to give more starters the opportunity to use this option.

277221.explanation: Yes. It is formally established that at least 25% of the total social rental housing must be allocated to house seekers who have a connection with Soest/Soesterberg. In practice, this is already the case for about 70% of the housing allocations.

277222.explanation: If the percentage of social rental housing is increased, project developers will find it hard to get a business case to work. This could cause stagnation in new construction. No one benefits from that. Social rental homes are mainly built by housing corporations, and they must be given space to do so.

277223.explanation: Yes, but only if it fits the neighborhood and does not harm the quality of the living environment and the village character of Soest. Then it should be possible to allow more than 6 building stories for housing in Soest and/or Soesterberg.

277224.explanation: There are already options for the municipality to receive financial support for transforming vacant office buildings into housing. As the municipality of Soest, we should definitely take advantage of this to realize more homes!

277228.explanation: In our opinion, the maximum speed on non-through roads should be reduced to 30 kilometers per hour. On main roads, the maximum speed can remain 50 kilometers per hour but dangerous spots should be assessed to improve safety.

277229.explanation: GGS wants all residents to feel involved in Soest and Soesterberg and to have influence on their living environment. Good methods exist, such as lotteries, to ensure a cross-section of residents. This selection can discuss a defined topic as a Citizens' Assembly and come to an advice.

277231.explanation: GGS wants a district alderman for Soesterberg. This way, the residents of Soesterberg get a direct point of contact with the municipality. We also strengthen cooperation and ensure that decisions truly align with the wishes and concerns of residents.

277232.explanation: There is no general legal obligation to keep public lists of meetings. We do not find such a 'lobby register' necessary at the municipal level. The possibility for a resident to obtain administrative information is, in our opinion, regulated via the Open Government Act (WOO).

277233.explanation: There is already a lot of vacancy within the existing shopping areas in Soest and Soesterberg. That vacancy will only increase if we allow more shops outside the existing shopping areas.

277234.explanation: The transition from fossil energy use to energy from renewable sources is necessary but not an easy task. We will do our utmost to achieve this. We support entrepreneurs who want to do this jointly.

277236.explanation: We want to introduce 'diftar' for waste collection as soon as possible. But some steps still have to be taken before it can actually be implemented. Very practical — a system for residual waste in apartment blocks, and: when can our waste service RMN implement this system?

277238.explanation: For GGS the polder is an essential part of the village character of Soest. We protect this area against urbanization. We will have to cooperate with farmers to realize the agrarian natural landscape. The Province of Utrecht will also have to contribute financially.

277239.explanation: Our village must remain rich in accessible parks, playgrounds, and green spaces where people can walk, exercise, play, and meet each other. We also focus on a robust green urban network that connects our village to the surrounding nature.

277241.explanation: GGS supports public concerts from a permanent bandstand, for example on the Van Weedestraat shopping promenade. It is important that there are places where people can pleasantly meet each other, such as a bandstand in a central location.

277242.explanation: A healthy green living environment for our youth is important. Therefore, GGS supports providing schoolyards with more plants and trees. GGS strives for a Soest that maintains and strengthens its green character — for ourselves and for generations to come.

277243.explanation: A healthy, green living environment is important for relaxation and well-being. 'Green' spaces to walk, cycle, play, and exercise. Places where you can meet people. We want to keep Soest livable and affordable, with clean air, green spaces, and biodiversity.

277245.explanation: More street lighting can contribute to safety and livability but also has drawbacks for nature and people’s health. Therefore, GGS does not advocate more lighting per se, but better lighting.

277247.explanation: It seems simple, but it makes little sense to develop new housing plans if we do not execute what is already planned: plans for more than 1,000 homes. Let's build those first. The green character of Soest is unique, and we cherish that. GGS protects the open polder.

277248.explanation: This always depends on necessity and possibilities.

277249.explanation: Experience shows that temporary homes usually cause delays for the permanent developments planned.

277250.explanation: For Van Weedestraat, it was previously decided to set part of it as one-way traffic. This was at the express request of entrepreneurs in the area. That seems logical to us, and we see no reason to change it.

277251.explanation: There are rules for this that must be complied with. GGS does support the existing minimum income schemes to which residents with an income up to 130% of the social assistance norm are entitled. We ensure that these schemes are retained.

277252.explanation: Cyclists must be better protected. On roads where a separate, segregated cycling path is not possible, it must be clearly indicated which part of the road is intended for cyclists. At the same time, we invest in an extensive cycling and walking network with safe, attractive routes (including along the Eem).

277253.explanation: Slow traffic in built-up areas should always have priority over motorized vehicles. Different street layouts, 30 km zones, and good cycle routes help with this without limiting car accessibility.

277254.explanation: Where necessary and where possible, more parking spaces should be created. But only according to the current parking standards of the municipality of Soest.

277255.explanation: Informal caregivers deserve appreciation and support, for example, gift vouchers. Organizations such as SWOS, COSBO, Support Point for Informal Care, and Foundation Balance play an important role in this.

277256.explanation: A village pass is effective as a participation tool and strengthens equal opportunities, especially for children and families. But the management must remain simple; for GGS this is a condition to introduce a village pass in our municipality.

277257.explanation: In our municipality, residents with debt can turn to the municipality, the free walk-in center GOOS, SchuldHulpMaatje, and Welfare. GGS believes the municipality should actively focus on education, early detection, and cooperation with organizations to tackle financial problems in time.

277258.explanation: If there is a need for such a center, we should decide per neighborhood. However, the costs of a new center often prove to be very high. We find it important that meetings take place within the neighborhood but also that we first examine whether existing buildings can be used multifunctionally.

277259.explanation: Most fast cycling routes are in development or being built by the province. GGS advocates for safe and comfortable cycle routes in rural areas and to connect Soesterberg with surrounding villages and cities. This makes it easier to travel sustainably and healthily.

277260.explanation: Small events can suffice with fewer demands or rules. GGS wants to provide space for local residents’ initiatives, small events aimed at meeting each other, where nuisance, noise, and health are not concerns.

277261.explanation: We support and encourage residents’ initiatives that contribute to a lively, engaged, and social village. Space for local residents’ initiatives gives everyone the chance to think along about the direction we want our village to take.

277262.explanation: GGS is currently waiting to see what happens with the Wind on Isselt project and further coordination in planning. Together with the Province and six surrounding municipalities, we are working on sufficient wind and solar energy – with emphasis on rooftop solar – to achieve our climate goals for 2030.

277263.explanation: Provided there is better accessibility for residents and more efficient use of resources can be realized.

277264.explanation: The municipality of Zeist still has a reception center on the border with Soesterberg. The COA and the municipality of Zeist seem to be considering disputing the agreed end date. This impacts Soesterberg.

277265.explanation: We do not consider this a task for the municipality. There are national organizations that offer exit programs.

277267.explanation: The sooner an established status holder has a job, the sooner that person or their family will integrate.

277268.explanation: Plans for 1,000 homes are still ready. Let's first get those done as quickly and affordably as possible before the municipality buys (more) land.

277213.explanation: Raising taxes is the easy way out. Low costs for our residents and entrepreneurs remain our starting point. That living expenses may be higher elsewhere is never an excuse to increase taxes for our residents.

277214.explanation: We are against giving priority to status holders in the allocation of social rental housing. Social rental housing is for those who need it, with fair and equal rules for everyone; status holders therefore simply join the other residents of Soest in the waiting list.

277215.explanation: No patronizing behavior behind the front door and no rules that you cannot enforce. You can provide information about responsible heating behavior so that one's pleasures do not become others' burdens.

277216.explanation: Sports and culture provide enjoyment, connection, and self-confidence. They contribute to a healthy, resilient, and involved society. Every child must be able to participate. Sports connect and strengthen. Money should not be a barrier.

277217.explanation: More visible enforcement officers means quicker intervention and a safer village. Safety requires clear boundaries, visible enforcement, and attention to prevention. We opt for a municipality where troublemakers are dealt with and neighborhood officers and enforcement officers can act quickly.

277218.explanation: We do not encourage the establishment of new coffee shops due to the health consequences and the sometimes experienced nuisance in the area. There appears to be no demand for new coffee shops. If such demand does exist, we depend on zoning plans or permission.

277219.explanation: If the police also see added value in this, we are in favor; after all, we do not decide on police capacity deployment. A well-known local police officer in the neighborhood increases trust and safety. Close by and approachable.

277220.explanation: We are proud to help first-time buyers with the starter loan. But a loan must be responsible and not an unbearable burden. The starter must still have money left to live on. €30,000 is therefore a fine and responsible amount.

277221.explanation: Those who live, work, or grew up here deserve a fair chance at a home in their own village.

277222.explanation: No rigid percentages. We build what is needed: affordable, appropriate, and with attention to housing flow.

277223.explanation: High-rise buildings only if they fit. Quality and village character take precedence over numbers. But where it fits, it’s a shame to block more homes with a rigid rule.

277224.explanation: Smart use of vacancy is good, but subsidies are not necessary as far as we are concerned. We expect that a developer can still earn enough when converting an office to housing, especially if we remove unnecessary rules.

277228.explanation: We opt for tailored safety. 30 km/h where necessary, but not as a general mandatory measure. We must also keep an eye on accessibility and traffic flow in our village. Furthermore, 30 km/h roads must also be designed as such, which costs millions.

277229.explanation: Consultation is good, but decisions are made by elected representatives. Regular participation with local residents must happen during projects. And not through an arbitrary group that does not live nearby.

277231.explanation: We support having a fixed contact person per neighborhood. A village or district alderman fits this role — close by, visible, and involved.

277232.explanation: We are for transparency but against unnecessary rules. Let's not impose big city rules on our village. Transparency yes, extra paperwork no. Openness without bureaucracy.

277233.explanation: We want to invest in our shopping areas because shopkeepers form the foundation of our local economy and contribute to a vibrant village life. Fragmentation does not help strengthen shopping areas. However, we are open to a supermarket outside the regular shopping areas.

277234.explanation: Entrepreneurs collaborating on energy deserve space — smart, local, and future-oriented. We can use national schemes to stimulate this.

277236.explanation: DIFTAR punishes families and increases illegal dumping. We choose affordable waste policy with only measures that demonstrably work. Paying per kilo of waste is not an option for us.

277238.explanation: We see no reason to damage the character of the polder. We are frugal with taxpayers' money and therefore only do what is necessary.

277239.explanation: Our village already looks beautiful because we invest significantly in high maintenance standards. Well-maintained greenery makes neighborhoods more beautiful, safer, and nicer to live in. We will keep it that way.

277241.explanation: Nice initiatives come from the community. We do not see a bandstand as a municipal task. If an entrepreneur sees opportunities for a bandstand, we would encourage that.

277242.explanation: We already do this, because more greenery means more play, less heat, and healthier children.

277243.explanation: We promote sports, walking, and cycling in the green areas of Soest and Soesterberg. Free sports facilities in the neighborhood are good for health and social interaction. Therefore, we support sufficient play and sports areas in every neighborhood to keep young and old active.

277245.explanation: We opt for tailored safety. More lighting where necessary for safety, but we must also consider nature and counter unnecessary light pollution.

277247.explanation: We stand for a healthy balance between housing and greenery. We do not choose new large-scale expansion locations if it comes at the expense of our village character. We are open to small-scale expansions at the edges of our village that can be logically integrated.

277248.explanation: Buildings that we know the municipality will need permanently are better to buy than rent. Renting is more expensive in the long term, and we handle public money responsibly.

277249.explanation: We support fast housing construction, but temporary homes must go through the same procedures as permanent ones. Therefore, we want to focus on accelerating permanent housing construction.

277250.explanation: There is no space to create a square on Van Weedestraat unless you completely close the street. We are not in favor of that.

277251.explanation: Those who can work must participate. Those who refuse to work receive an appropriate sanction. Rights come with responsibilities.

277252.explanation: We prioritize safety, but there are many space claimers in public spaces. Where it fits, we want to explore possibilities, but it will not be feasible everywhere.

277253.explanation: The number of traffic lights in our municipality is so limited that cyclists rarely have to wait long. Where the light changes to green for cyclists earlier, cars wait longer. We oppose discouraging car use and are in favor of smooth car traffic flow.

277254.explanation: We support realistic parking standards so residents can park their cars in front of their houses and near shopping areas. Most households need their first and second car, and if parking near shops is not possible, residents go to neighboring municipalities for shopping.

277255.explanation: Caregivers deserve our support. We actively inform residents about support options for caregiving, such as respite care and (pre)caregiver housing. So they can continue their important role without becoming overburdened themselves.

277256.explanation: There are already minimum support schemes and youth can use the Youth Fund for Sports & Culture. We are frugal with taxpayers’ money and therefore only do what is necessary.

277257.explanation: Prevention is better than cure. Early help prevents debts.

277258.explanation: Social interaction is important, but we have many neighborhoods that can sometimes share a meeting place. Having a separate meeting center in every neighborhood costs a lot of money while there will be insufficient visitors.

277259.explanation: We support fast cycling routes but also keep core tasks where they belong. We see an important role here for the province because a cycling path does not stop at the municipal border. So regional cooperation with provincial funding — we are happy to contribute.

277260.explanation: We advocate for simple permits for small (cultural) events and neighborhood gatherings, with less bureaucracy and faster procedures. We must cherish these initiatives and not hold them back with unnecessary procedures.

277261.explanation: Involving residents is good, but the council remains responsible for our budget. There are already financial opportunities for citizen initiatives. We do not need extra bureaucracy for this.

277262.explanation: We are against large wind turbines and choose solar panels on existing buildings. We are open to exploring, together with the province, the possibilities for a small nuclear reactor. And innovative initiatives like heat from surface water (including ponds).

277263.explanation: We support having one welfare organization for all target groups. In our municipality there are many organizations working for the welfare of residents. But it is sometimes unclear where to go, and there is too much overlap. We will make support clearer, more accessible, and more effective.

277264.explanation: Directly next to the core of Soesterberg lies the Kamp van Zeist, where about 400 asylum seekers are housed. As far as we are concerned, this reception facility explicitly counts towards the municipality of Soest. Therefore, we are not responsible for additional accommodation within our municipal borders.

277265.explanation: Outside the municipality, initiatives already exist to help sex workers find other jobs if they wish. We are not in favor of taking on additional tasks that are unnecessary; we want to be careful with taxpayers' money.

277267.explanation: Especially the status holders must make an effort to find paid employment as quickly as possible (own responsibility). The municipality supports this, but expects commitment and social participation. Rights come with obligations. If that commitment is absent, sanctions will follow.

277268.explanation: The municipality is not a project developer. We stand for cautious and sensible land policy.

277213.explanation: Cuts from the national government must not be shifted onto homeowners. It is mistakenly believed sometimes that homeowners are automatically affluent and can therefore be taxed more. According to POS, this is unjustified. We are therefore very reluctant to increase property taxes.

277214.explanation: POS believes social rental housing should be fairly distributed and given priority to home seekers from our own municipality. Priority for status holders is difficult to explain to young people and starters from our own villages who have been waiting for a home for years.

277215.explanation: We are aware of the impact wood burning can have on air quality. However, in our opinion, a ban goes too far. We prefer good information and the use of a burning guide to make residents aware of when they should or should not use their wood stove.

277216.explanation: Sports are essential for being and staying healthy. We believe it is important that sports remain affordable, also for those with less income. In special circumstances, a request can therefore be made to, for example, the Youth Fund for Sports and Culture.

277217.explanation: Many citizens in the Netherlands increasingly feel unsafe, including in Soest. Visible presence and approachability of BOAs (public order officers) and police help increase safety and ensure nuisance and crime can be better addressed.

277218.explanation: Coffeeshops make it easier to obtain (soft) drugs. Easy availability lowers the threshold to use. Moreover, coffeeshops can lead to extra crime and nuisance in the area. We are therefore against establishing new coffeeshops in our municipality.

277219.explanation: The neighborhood-oriented deployment of neighborhood police officers must be strengthened. A weekly consultation hour ensures the neighborhood officer is easily approachable for residents and helps the officer understand what is happening in the neighborhood.

277220.explanation: POS believes starter loans should move with the housing market. The current maximum does not sufficiently align with house prices and thus helps starters too little in buying their first home.

277221.explanation: POS focuses on the current residents of Soest and Soesterberg so they can remain living in their village. A motion by POS and others to establish a priority scheme for social rental housing for Soest and Soesterberg residents was adopted by the municipal council in 2021.

277222.explanation: Building must meet demand: affordable homes, whether for purchase or rent. New construction should now consist of 30% social rental, 5% middle rental, 15% social purchase, and 15% affordable purchase. A larger share of social rental housing would come at the expense of other housing, which we do not consider desirable.

277223.explanation: It must always be carefully considered whether – and if so, what kind of – construction is appropriate in each location. This applies to both Soesterberg and Soest. We therefore support setting maximum building heights in parts of our municipality to preserve the village-like and green character.

277224.explanation: The housing shortage is large and requires creative solutions. Converting offices into homes can contribute to this. In addition, POS supports smart combinations of working and living, for example on the TBS terrain in Soest.

277228.explanation: Traffic safety is very important to POS. On through roads within built-up areas, 50 km/h can remain the standard in our view. Speeders must be addressed. In residential neighborhoods themselves, we support the introduction of 30 km/h zones.

277229.explanation: POS believes it is important that residents have real influence over major decisions. A citizen's assembly can help hear different voices and make plans better align with what lives within society.

277231.explanation: POS originated in Soesterberg and is very familiar with the perceived distance between the village and the municipal government. Having a fixed alderman as a contact person for Soesterberg can strengthen connection and trust, without detracting attention from the rest of the municipality.

277232.explanation: Soest is not Brussels. Consultations with organizations and companies are part of good local governance. Transparency arises mainly through public decision-making, debate in the municipal council, and a council that properly exercises its controlling role. Keeping lists primarily leads to more work and bureaucracy.

277233.explanation: More flexibility for shops outside shopping areas is needed. Due to current policy, these are often not possible in logical locations at the edges of our villages, while they precisely contribute to the wishes of our residents. Think specifically of the desire for a supermarket in Soesterberg North.

277234.explanation: POS supports local entrepreneurs who want to jointly generate sustainable energy. By supporting initiatives from the grassroots, we strengthen sustainability and local entrepreneurship.

277236.explanation: POS is against diftar (pay-per-kilo garbage system). Experiences in other municipalities, such as Zeewolde, show that paying per kilogram of residual waste leads to more illegal dumping and sometimes results in unfair waste charges between single-person and multi-person households. We choose good sorting at the source, as is currently done.

277238.explanation: POS sees agricultural nature management as a possible instrument, but not as a standard solution for the entire polder. It must be considered where it fits, with attention to nature and agriculture, without blocking possibilities for future housing development behind, for example, the Schans.

277239.explanation: Well-maintained parks, playgrounds, and green spaces are important for livability, health, and social interaction, which contributes to the well-being of our residents. POS believes the municipality must invest sufficiently in this.

277241.explanation: POS finds it important that cultural initiatives fit what residents need and want. A permanent bandstand can be valuable but is not an end in itself. Choices about this must be made together with residents.

277242.explanation: Green schoolyards make the living environment healthier and more pleasant. POS sees this as a logical investment in youth, greenery, and livability.

277243.explanation: Free sports and exercise facilities in new residential areas contribute to health, social interaction, and livability. We do wonder, though, whether this fits every new neighborhood. Not every new residential area is equally large or always has the same population composition.

277245.explanation: Good street lighting increases the feeling of safety, especially for women and girls who travel alone in the evening. POS believes the public space must be safe and accessible for everyone. Where necessary, street lighting should be installed.

277247.explanation: POS sees the polder behind De Schans as landscape-wise less valuable and thus a logical location for housing. Affordable homes with priority for Soest residents can be realized here, with attention to nature, landscape, and good integration.

277248.explanation: When building homes, investment in social cohesion must also take place. POS believes the municipality itself must invest in social infrastructure, such as community centers and cultural centers, so growth goes hand in hand with social interaction and culture.

277249.explanation: Temporary housing can sometimes help but is not a standard solution. POS wants to assess what is appropriate per location, with attention to livability, support, and the final plans for the site.

277250.explanation: At Van Weedestraat there is already a car-free square near the HEMA. Blocking or adjusting the adjacent one-way street, according to POS, adds little extra value and can actually worsen access to shops.

277251.explanation: POS believes situations vary greatly. People who can work should do so as much as possible. But not everyone can simply look for work due to health or caregiving duties, for example. The municipality must focus on tailored solutions and support, not automatic benefit cuts.

277252.explanation: Good and safe bike paths in rural areas increase traffic safety for cyclists. POS believes that cars and cyclists should be separated where possible so everyone can travel safely.

277253.explanation: POS wants traffic lights to enable safe and smooth traffic for all road users. Sometimes quicker green lights for cyclists can help, but this must be weighed per intersection to prevent traffic jams and unsafe situations.

277254.explanation: Many residents depend on cars for shopping and work. In new construction, we systematically see too few parking spaces created. POS believes parking must not become a problem due to overly strict standards and advocates sufficient parking spaces at homes and shops.

277255.explanation: Informal caregivers who care for sick parents, partners, or children with disabilities are indispensable. By supporting them well, they can sustain their care and the municipality prevents people from becoming dependent more quickly on more expensive professional care.

277256.explanation: Participation in activities should not depend on financial means. POS supports a village pass for residents with low incomes, with a careful and fair income test.

277257.explanation: Good information about financial problems helps residents to prevent or address debts early. POS believes the municipality must continue to invest in this, because prevention is better than cure.

277258.explanation: POS supports meeting places in neighborhoods, but not a one-size-fits-all solution. Each neighborhood should assess what is needed and feasible to stimulate social interaction.

277259.explanation: Fast cycling routes make biking safer and more attractive for commuting. POS believes the municipality should invest in this, with attention to safety, the environment, and good integration.

277260.explanation: Small events contribute to social interaction and cohesion. POS believes the municipality should relax regulations to encourage initiatives from residents instead of discouraging them. The government should have more trust in the strength and responsibility of society.

277261.explanation: As a residents' party of Soest and Soesterberg, we believe residents themselves should be able to make plans for their neighborhood. By reserving a budget per neighborhood and letting residents choose which plan will be executed, you strengthen involvement, social cohesion, and trust in the local government.

277262.explanation: Locations such as the Isselt industrial area may be considered for sustainable energy, but only if the effects on local residents, landscape, and livability are acceptable. The safety and health of local residents must not be compromised. POS does not support automatic placement.

277263.explanation: POS sees that many welfare organizations work alongside each other, which does not always benefit oversight and effectiveness. Cooperation or consolidation can help, as long as the interests of residents and good service remain leading.

277264.explanation: In fact, there is already an asylum seekers center near Soesterberg: on Zeist territory at Kamp van Zeist, 400 asylum seekers reside. They depend on the facilities in Soesterberg. Therefore, POS does not want additional asylum seekers centers in Soesterberg.

277265.explanation: Those who want to leave sex work must be able to count on support from the municipality. POS stands for freedom of choice, guidance, and a safe route to other work.

277267.explanation: The municipality has a role in guiding people with a distance to the labor market toward employment. POS believes status holders should be included in this, without distinction compared to others.

277268.explanation: POS believes the municipality should actively purchase land to maintain control over housing construction. This allows for steering affordable housing, the right mix of housing types, and appropriate facilities in the neighborhood.

277213.explanation: D66 manages tax money carefully. At this moment, it is not necessary to increase the property tax (OZB). If it becomes necessary in the future to balance the budget while protecting the most vulnerable residents, we are open for that discussion.

277214.explanation: Not yet. According to the new coalition agreement, priority will be abolished once there are enough homes. Priority currently exists to relieve asylum seekers centers and prevent bottlenecks. Once that is in order, priority can be removed.

277215.explanation: Fireplaces and wood stoves may remain, but D66 encourages social and responsible use. So, no burning during orange alert.

277216.explanation: D66 focuses on youth and education. Every child participates, even if parents have little money.

277217.explanation: D66 does not believe that more enforcement officers (BOA’s) automatically leads to more safety. We work on prevention through youth workers, good cooperation, and a safe living environment.

277218.explanation: D66 is not in favor of a fundamental ban on new coffeeshops but sets clear conditions for safety, health, and livability.

277219.explanation: D66 encourages easy access to the neighborhood police officer. It contributes to a sense of community and safety in the neighborhood and makes prevention easier.

277220.explanation: D66 wants starters in Soest to really have a chance. If a higher starter loan helps with that and is financially feasible, we should seriously explore that option. Moreover, house prices continue to rise, so it makes sense for the loan amount to increase accordingly.

277221.explanation: D66 Soest chooses a regional approach. Therefore, we support a priority rule for the Amersfoort region and not only for Soest. This gives Soest residents a better chance of social rental housing in our region.

277222.explanation: Everyone deserves a nice place to live. D66 maintains a 30% social housing share and subsequently a good build-up mainly of affordable owner-occupied and rental homes, so that growth is possible.

277223.explanation: D66 realizes that space is scarce and that building upward offers opportunities. We assess height per location with attention to livability, nature, and spatial quality.

277224.explanation: D66 supports converting vacant offices into housing but believes no municipal subsidy is needed for this.

277228.explanation: D66 supports 30 km/h as the standard speed in residential areas. Where necessary for emergency services and bus lines, 50 km/h remains possible.

277229.explanation: D66 believes input from society improves political decision-making. We therefore support more and better participation, for example in the form of a citizens' assembly.

277231.explanation: D66 understands the need for better representation and closeness in Soesterberg but believes this applies to all aldermen and councilors.

277232.explanation: D66 stands for open and transparent governance. Via the Open Government Act, this data can already be requested. Therefore, we do not see the need for an additional list to be maintained.

277233.explanation: D66 wishes our entrepreneurs and residents vibrant and well-visited shopping areas. We choose strong, compact centers and careful use of space.

277234.explanation: D66 wants to achieve sustainability goals. We support entrepreneurs who want to generate sustainable energy.

277236.explanation: D66 wants to promote the reuse of valuable materials and reduce waste. Proper separation is therefore important, and we gladly reward that with lower costs.

277238.explanation: D66 supports cooperation with farmers for a greener polder landscape but does not support the idea that the municipality should give money directly to farmers, unless the work is done on behalf of the municipality.

277239.explanation: D66 wants every neighborhood to have space for young and old to relax outdoors. The municipality of Soest ensures good maintenance. We see potential to introduce more diversity, also together with residents, for a green, healthy, and attractive living environment.

277241.explanation: D66 supports art and culture as a bonding agent in our society. We already have several beautiful places for art, meetings, and events in Soest and Soesterberg. We believe that is enough.

277242.explanation: D66 has long advocated for greener schoolyards. We gladly support schools that choose this. It would also be nice if these schoolyards were open on weekends for neighborhood children to play.

277243.explanation: D66 supports the establishment of free sports facilities in new residential areas because this contributes to health, livability, and equal opportunities to exercise.

277245.explanation: D66 is not in favor of more street lighting as a general rule but does support smart, safe, and nature-friendly lighting (LED) where needed.

277247.explanation: We are in favor of a well-founded and public plan for housing construction in Soest. We do not exclude anything upfront, but soil and water conditions are leading, and broad consultation is a given. Additionally, we want to preserve Soest’s skyline as seen from the Eem.

277248.explanation: D66 supports good cultural and social facilities but not necessarily buying municipal buildings. We choose tailored solutions, cooperation, and financially sound choices.

277249.explanation: D66 is open to temporary housing where it is logical and feasible. This does not have to be at all future building locations. We opt for customization, speed where possible, and care where necessary.

277250.explanation: D66 chooses vibrant shopping areas. We see a car-free square on Van Weedestraat as part of a broader vision for an attractive center. But we do not believe it must be realized.

277251.explanation: D66 chooses activation, support, and offering perspective. Not punishment. People receiving social assistance benefits can contribute to our society not only through work but also in other ways.

277252.explanation: If that proves to be the best option, at least. D66 primarily wants to invest in the safety of cyclists in rural areas.

277253.explanation: D66 promotes cycling, walking, and public transport. We opt for better flow for cyclists and smart safety solutions. Traffic lights for cyclists and pedestrians should therefore turn green faster.

277254.explanation: D66 chooses livability, sustainable mobility, and tailored solutions to parking pressure — not standard expansion of parking spaces.

277255.explanation: D66 wants to support informal caregivers. We find the word 'support' very important. Informal caregivers do their 'work' out of love for their loved ones. A helping hand can make a big difference.

277256.explanation: D66 chooses connection and participation. We are therefore positive about increasing accessibility for residents with low incomes. We encourage effective, accessible support that really helps — this can be via a card or existing arrangements.

277257.explanation: D66 does not want to patronize residents, but wants to empower them through good information and education. We are therefore positive about continuing to provide education on financial problems to prevent debt. Debt greatly affects people's well-being.

277258.explanation: D66 supports meeting places in every neighborhood, but it does not always have to be a community center in each neighborhood.

277259.explanation: D66 wants more cycling and fewer cars. To reduce parking pressure and promote health, well-being, and a healthy green environment. Together with our neighboring municipalities, we invest in fast cycling routes.

277260.explanation: D66 believes small events contribute to community spirit and fit our idea of 'Coming Home'. We want more tailored options instead of a single set of rules.

277261.explanation: D66 strongly believes in the strength and skills of society and gladly gives people the space to make and implement plans together.

277262.explanation: D66 promotes the generation of sustainable energy. The municipality of Amersfoort permits two wind turbines at the Isselt location. It is efficient and possible to place two more wind turbines on the adjacent Soester territory, provided the turbines are 50% owned by Soester residents.

277263.explanation: D66 encourages strong cooperation between welfare organizations but does not support merging all organizations into one large institution. We want residents to have choices and trust the knowledge of our organizations.

277264.explanation: Soesterberg has made sufficient contributions to asylum reception in recent years. D66 chooses a careful, humane, and participatory approach, where a location is only chosen after research and consultation.

277265.explanation: D66 gladly offers voluntary help and support for sex workers who want another job but does not support policies that actively push them out of the profession.

277267.explanation: D66 believes in the strength and potential of people. If everyone can participate, that benefits us all collectively. Language education, housing, and paid work accelerate integration and contribute to society. Therefore, a temporary investment for sustainable results. That is what D66 chooses.

277268.explanation: D66 does not rule out that the municipality buys land for housing construction. We choose direction, affordability, and customization and use land policy only when it helps to realize good affordable homes.

277213.explanation: The CDA wants to keep costs for residents and entrepreneurs as low as possible. According to the CDA, a higher property tax does not fit with this.

277214.explanation: The CDA wants to prioritize residents of Soest and Soesterberg. If recognized refugees live there, they are also residents and, according to the CDA, should be given priority.

277215.explanation: The CDA believes that the use of open fireplaces and wood stoves should remain allowed. Residents must burn responsibly with dry wood. In case of nuisance, the CDA urges neighbors to talk to each other.

277216.explanation: The CDA believes every child has the right to a healthy childhood with sufficient exercise. Therefore, the municipality should continue to pay for sports club memberships for children from low-income families.

277217.explanation: The CDA wants municipal enforcement officers visible in every neighborhood. Therefore, according to the CDA, it is good if more municipal officers work on safety.

277218.explanation: The CDA does not want new coffeeshops. Existing coffeeshops may only sell hash and weed, no other drugs. The municipality must strictly control, especially to prevent sales to minors and provide good education to youth about the risks.

277219.explanation: The CDA wants residents of Soest and Soesterberg to maintain the opportunity to speak with the neighborhood police officer, for example through a weekly consultation hour.

277220.explanation: The CDA believes the municipality must first build affordable housing. If the starter loan from 2024 is underused, the reason must be investigated. A higher amount might then be an option.

277221.explanation: The CDA wants residents of Soest and Soesterberg to continue receiving priority for social rental housing. This way, people can stay in their own neighborhood, close to family and acquaintances.

277222.explanation: The CDA wants to meet the agreements for 2024–2030. That means at least 30% social rental housing, which amounts to 485 new social rental homes. Additionally, the CDA wants enough mid-range rentals and owner-occupied homes for a good housing mix.

277223.explanation: The CDA is open to new residential towers. Additionally, the CDA wants to add extra floors to existing buildings to create more homes without using extra space.

277224.explanation: The CDA supports converting vacant offices into homes. Municipal subsidies are only needed if these homes cannot be realized otherwise.

277228.explanation: The CDA wants to keep current policy. In residential neighborhoods, 30 km/h is already the norm. On through roads, such as Koningsweg and Dalweg, the CDA does not want to introduce this speed limit.

277229.explanation: The CDA does not want an additional randomly selected advisory group. In Soest, there is already a lottery every four years for residents to participate in the council. There is also a youth council. For council members, maintaining contact with residents is a core part of their role as representatives.

277231.explanation: The CDA believes Soesterberg should have contact with all aldermen. This is more efficient and ensures that every alderman takes Soesterberg's interests into account.

277232.explanation: The CDA finds a public list of organizations and companies that meet with aldermen unnecessary. According to the CDA, such a lobby register does not increase transparency.

277233.explanation: The CDA wants to strengthen existing shopping centers. More shops outside these areas can result in fewer shops and less vibrancy in the current centers.

277234.explanation: The CDA wants to continue helping entrepreneurs with subsidies for research into sustainable plans. The CDA also wants businesses to share electricity and coordinate their consumption to reduce pressure on the power grid.

277236.explanation: The CDA does not want extra costs for more residual waste. The CDA chooses good education to reduce waste. Sometimes this is not possible, for example with (incontinence) diapers. Then higher costs are unfair.

277238.explanation: The CDA believes farmers have an important role in the landscape and should have space to keep their businesses healthy. Farmers have the best interests for their business and the polder. The municipality should maintain contact to jointly examine what is needed without pre-imposed plans or conditions.

277239.explanation: The CDA wants residents to be more involved in maintaining parks, playgrounds, and public gardens. By supporting citizen initiatives, involvement in the neighborhood grows. This can sometimes mean more or less funding is needed.

277241.explanation: The CDA has no fixed position on this. If residents, through a citizen initiative, show that a permanent bandstand strengthens the community, then this can be an option.

277242.explanation: The CDA wants to renew the education housing plan every two years. This also includes the school environment. During implementation, attention is paid to greening schoolyards, such as adding plants and trees.

277243.explanation: The CDA wants new neighborhoods to be carefully planned with appropriate facilities. These include out-of-hours GP services, meeting places, and sufficient outdoor spaces where residents can meet, play, and exercise outside.

277245.explanation: The CDA wants to ensure a safe living environment. Therefore, the CDA advocates for additional street lighting in dark and unsafe spots in the villages.

277247.explanation: The CDA wants a residential area in the polder behind De Schans with a mix of social rental, mid-range rental, and affordable owner-occupied homes. This should include good facilities such as meeting places, shared play areas, and good connections to the village.

277248.explanation: The CDA believes a meeting place in every neighborhood is important to promote more contact among residents. These places can be used by the municipality and social organizations for information, activities, and as emergency shelters during outages of other services such as electricity.

277249.explanation: The CDA wants to build more homes as soon as possible. Temporary housing can help, but the CDA prefers to immediately build permanent dwellings.

277250.explanation: The CDA has no fixed position on this. If residents demonstrate, through a citizen initiative, a good plan for a car-free square at Van Weedestraat, the CDA is open to it.

277251.explanation: The CDA advocates for a municipality based on trust even in the social domain, with less control. Not everyone on social assistance can work or is obligated to seek employment. However, the CDA calls on unemployed people to voluntarily contribute to society where possible.

277252.explanation: The CDA wants safe, accessible, and sustainable transportation options. Good cycling and walking paths, public transport, and safe roads ensure accessibility and participation. If separated bike lanes in rural areas increase safety, they can be implemented.

277253.explanation: The CDA wants safe, accessible, and sustainable transportation options. Traffic lights should be well-coordinated for all road users so that traffic flow for cyclists, cars, and pedestrians is safe and smooth.

277254.explanation: The CDA wants to assess parking on a neighborhood-by-neighborhood basis. Each area will be examined to determine what is needed. More parking spaces may be a solution, as well as encouraging shared transport.

277255.explanation: The CDA wants better support for informal caregivers. For example, with respite care and sufficient availability of day activities. If more funding is needed for this, the CDA believes the municipality should provide it.

277256.explanation: The CDA wants every resident to be able to participate in activities in Soest. Various schemes already exist for people with low incomes. The CDA supports initiatives from the community that make participation easier.

277257.explanation: The CDA wants residents facing financial difficulties to be helped quickly and humanely. With a single clear desk and one consistent support worker, they receive assistance to overcome this difficult period as soon as possible.

277258.explanation: The CDA believes a meeting place in every neighborhood is important to foster contact among residents. The municipality and social organizations can use these places for information and as emergency shelters when other services, such as electricity, fail.

277259.explanation: The CDA wants safe, accessible, and sustainable transportation options. Good cycling and walking paths, public transport, and safe roads are important for accessibility. Fast cycling routes must connect well with each other and be addressed together with the province.

277260.explanation: The CDA wants fewer rules and more trust in residents. Small annual events should not have to apply for a new permit each time. According to the CDA, a simple notification is sufficient.

277261.explanation: The CDA primarily wants a municipality that works with residents to see what is needed in each neighborhood. Good ideas from residents should receive support. This can be done without fixing a predetermined budget per neighborhood but with room to assist plans that truly contribute to the area.

277262.explanation: The CDA believes there is no place for wind turbines within the municipality, but supports increasing the number of roofs producing solar energy. The site at Isselt (municipality of Amersfoort) is already designated for wind turbines, and this will continue.

277263.explanation: The CDA believes that each welfare organization has its own role. Combining everything into one organization is not wise in the CDA's view, because previous mergers in other sectors elsewhere often did not work well.

277264.explanation: The CDA does not want an asylum seekers' center in Soesterberg or in vulnerable neighborhoods. To comply with the dispersal law, the CDA only sees small-scale reception locations as a possible solution.

277265.explanation: The CDA believes that sex workers who want to stop and seek other jobs should receive help from the municipality to find new employment.

277267.explanation: The CDA wants status holders living in the municipality to participate as soon as possible. Therefore, the CDA wants to invest in good language education and assistance in finding paid work.

277268.explanation: The CDA wants the municipality to be able to buy land itself to build homes. This allows the municipality to provide social rental, mid-range rental, and affordable owner-occupied housing. The project at Dalweg shows that this works.

277214.explanation: Equal opportunities for everyone. There are many people looking for housing.

277215.explanation: Citizens can be responsible for responsible heating themselves.

277216.explanation: Sports are good for a healthy mind and a healthy body. Money should not be a barrier to participation.

277217.explanation: Being visible on the street creates a greater sense of safety and also works preventively.

277218.explanation: Drug use can lead to addictive behavior and does not promote public health.

277219.explanation: Narrows the gap between citizens and police. Also helps combat crime.

277220.explanation: With rising house prices, the maximum amount of the starter loan may increase to a maximum of 50,000 euros.

277221.explanation: We primarily build to meet the housing needs of our own residents.

277222.explanation: This ratio has been viewed too one-sidedly. If it is currently 23%, it would already be a great achievement to reach 35% social rental housing over the next four years.

277223.explanation: We advocate for the village character of Soest and Soesterberg.

277224.explanation: Of course we welcome the conversion of vacant office buildings into homes, but this must not be done with public funds.

277228.explanation: This ensures greater traffic safety and limits environmental damage.

277231.explanation: All aldermen serve all residents of our municipality.

277232.explanation: Pointless pastime, and what do you do with that list? Whose interest does this serve?

277233.explanation: It gives some liveliness to the neighborhoods.

277234.explanation: Where national subsidy schemes can be used, they certainly should be. Besides this, the municipality should not create its own scheme.

277238.explanation: Not a municipal task. But a provincial and national task.

277239.explanation: A lot is already being done. We do consider adoption by neighborhood or street.

277241.explanation: No significant demand has been demonstrated. Also, a bandstand, when not in use, can become a source of nuisance.

277242.explanation: The responsibility lies with the schools themselves.

277243.explanation: The initiative primarily lies with the neighborhood residents.

277245.explanation: More street lighting is needed where unsafe situations arise.

277247.explanation: More housing needs to be built. Not everything can be done within the city, so a place must be sought where many different types of homes can be constructed.

277248.explanation: The municipality is not a real estate trader.

277249.explanation: That is capital destruction and not a municipal task (think of housing corporations).

277250.explanation: We see no possibility for this.

277251.explanation: The municipality does not engage in income policy. That is regulated nationally.

277254.explanation: The number of cars per household has increased, so the number of parking spaces is insufficient.

277255.explanation: Informal caregivers are indispensable for the person involved and for society. Expressions of appreciation on behalf of society should not be missing.

277256.explanation: The municipality provides sufficient help and reduces stigma.

277257.explanation: Financial problems should be identified as early as possible.

277258.explanation: The municipality can facilitate, but responsibility should be placed with social organizations.

277260.explanation: It is good if fewer rules are introduced, especially for small events.

277261.explanation: Provided there is good consultation between citizens and the municipality.

277262.explanation: Our municipality is not suitable for wind turbines.

277263.explanation: Provided it does not come at the expense of quality. It prevents duplicate costs.

277264.explanation: Soesterberg already bears the 'burden' of many asylum seekers from the Zeist Camp.

277265.explanation: Then the municipality must first create an exit program with criteria.

277267.explanation: We desire maximum effort in this regard.

277268.explanation: The municipality is not a real estate trader.

277213.explanation: Agreed, if a budget is needed to invest in nature, energy transition, health, and social facilities. Lowering property tax rates means less budget and extra cuts in the coming years. The Party for the Animals supports differences in rates as an incentive to make houses more sustainable.

277214.explanation: Without priority, status holders are forced to continue living in asylum seekers' centers (AZCs). This blocks the flow, causing new asylum seekers to sleep in inhumane conditions (such as tents). Status holders have a disadvantage in registration time and therefore receive priority.

277215.explanation: Wood smoke produces a great deal of fine particulate matter and causes health problems for many residents. Soest provides a reporting point for wood smoke nuisance and informs residents about the disadvantages of burning wood.

277216.explanation: Sports must be accessible to everyone.

277217.explanation: Also important are youth workers, neighborhood parents, and community police officers; they are the eyes and ears of the neighborhood. It is better to identify potential problems at an early stage than to intervene when there is unsafe behavior.

277218.explanation: The Party for the Animals is not inherently for or against coffee shops. We believe municipalities are responsible for a safe living environment. Coffee shops can be part of this, provided they are well regulated and do not cause nuisance. The Party for the Animals does not want coffee shops near schools.

277219.explanation: It is pleasant for residents to know who their community police officer is and to be able to meet easily. Simpler and more personal contact can help residents feel safer and keep the police better informed.

277220.explanation: Starter loans can make homes more expensive in the municipality. The Party for the Animals wants to help starters find affordable housing but chooses other solutions, such as the municipality buying land itself to realize affordable homes.

277221.explanation: The Party for the Animals prefers to focus on more social rental housing and fair distribution rather than priority based on being a 'local resident.' Social rental housing is for those with the greatest housing need, not based on where you come from.

277222.explanation: Everyone has the right to affordable housing. Currently, too many people spend too much money on a roof over their heads. The Party for the Animals aims for 40% social rental housing in new developments.

277223.explanation: In the current housing shortage, high-rise buildings are one of the solutions for building homes. The municipality sets rules regarding high-rise buildings. Livability must not be compromised, high-rise buildings should fit the environment and have at least one green facade.

277224.explanation: Agreed, but with the caveat that homes must be sustainable and affordable, not primarily profitable for developers. No subsidy without clear agreements on this. As much housing as possible should be created smartly within existing buildings.

277228.explanation: Met 30 km/u binnen de bebouwde kom, uitgezonderd de Koningsweg, wordt de verkeersveiligheid en de luchtkwaliteit aanzienlijk verbeterd.

277229.explanation: In plaats van plannen maken voor inwoners, maken we plannen mét inwoners. Het burgerberaad, ook in kleinschaliger vorm (zoals wijkberaad of dorpsberaad), neemt een prominente plek in bij participatiebeleid. Dit zorgt voor meer draagvlak voor beslissingen.

277231.explanation: Inwoners uit Soesterberg ervaren vaak een afstand tot de gemeente, dit kan bijdragen tot het verkleinen van de afstand.

277232.explanation: Er komt een openbare lijst voor afspraken van wethouders. Bij raadsvoorstellen en uitgangspunten voor plannen wordt standaard vermeld welke betrokkenheid belangenbehartigers bij de voorstellen hebben gehad. Dit kan helpen het vertrouwen in de lokale politiek vergroten.

277233.explanation: Er is momenteel veel leegstand in het winkelgebied, dit is zonde van de ruimte. De beschikbare ruimte buiten het winkelgebied kan beter voor woningen gebruikt worden.

277234.explanation: De Partij voor de Dieren wil initiatieven voor het duurzaam opwekken van energie stimuleren en zorgen voor ondersteunend beleid.

277236.explanation: Minder afval is minder betalen. De PvdD is voorstander van het principe dat de vervuiler betaalt. De gemeente moet wel zorgen voor heldere regels rondom afvalscheiding. Zo wordt de burger geholpen om hun afval op de juiste manier te scheiden en kan de hoeveelheid restafval verminderd worden.
