277238.explanation: In de Soester polder verhogen we zoveel mogelijk met boeren de natuurwaarden en de biodiversiteit. Bijvoorbeeld door in te zetten op meer plas/drasgebied voor weidevogels, meer kruidenrijk grasland en het toevoegen van kleine landschapselementen. Natuurwaarden gaan voor opbrengstmaximalisatie.

277239.explanation: Eens, wanneer dit geld besteed wordt aan het vergroenen van deze plekken en voor de inkoop van gifvrije planten. Onderhoud moet wat ons betreft natuurvriendelijker, bijvoorbeeld door ecologisch maaibeleid dat insecten en andere dieren spaart.

277241.explanation: De Partij voor de Dieren staat open voor dit idee, indien inwoners hier in meerderheid behoefte aan hebben. En als een geschikte plek voor is, waar dit geen overlast voor mens, dier en natuur veroorzaakt. Een muziektent kan de dorpen, onder voorwaarden, verlevendigen.

277242.explanation: De gemeente stimuleert scholen en biedt financiële ondersteuning om groene schoolpleinen te realiseren waar kinderen vrij kunnen spelen. Als er plek is komt er ruimte voor schoolmoestuinen.

277243.explanation: Buiten kunnen sporten en bewegen is belangrijk voor welzijn en gezondheid en moet daarom aandacht krijgen bij de aanleg van nieuwe wijken. De aanleg van sportveldjes, wandel- en fietspaden in een fijne, groene omgeving stimuleren dat.

277245.explanation: Dieren hebben last van kunstlicht, hun ritme wordt erdoor verstoord en ze kunnen hierdoor in aantallen afnemen. We zijn terughoudend in het verlichten van parken en natuurgebieden en bij verlichten van gebouwen. Groen en water worden niet direct aangelicht en lampen stralen geen licht naar boven uit

277247.explanation: In de polder verhogen we de natuurwaarden en de biodiversiteit. De provincie wil meer natuur realiseren en kan hier onvoldoende ruimte voor vinden. De polders zijn hier uiterst geschikt voor. Door omhoogkomend kwelwater is bouwen in dit gebied duur en ingewikkeld wat huizen ook duur maakt.

277248.explanation: Indien nodig en gewenst biedt de gemeente ondersteuning aan culturele instellingen. Als de gemeente gebouwen koopt voor maatschappelijke doelen, moet zij daar ook passend beleid bij voeren: een redelijke, betaalbare huur, langjarige zekerheid en duidelijke keuzes. Anders is eigendom geen oplossing,

277249.explanation: Dit kan ten koste gaan van buurtparticipatie en het aanvragen van natuurvergunningen of bezwaren die hier tegen zijn ingediend. Het is alleen bespreekbaar indien het niet om een natuurgebied gaat en de woningen circulair en duurzaam zijn.

277250.explanation: De Partij voor de Dieren wil het verblijven in het winkelgebied aantrekkelijker maken en tevens autoverkeer terugdringen. Het autovrij maken van een deel van de Van Weedestraat zou hierbij helpen. Er komt zo ruimte voor meer groen en terrasjes. Ondernemers worden nauw bij zo’n plan betrokken.

277251.explanation: Participatie is meer dan betaald werk. Soest erkent ook niet-betaald werk als participatie, zoals vrijwilligerswerk en heeft een ruimhartige blik op de bijstandsgerechtigde.

277252.explanation: We onderzoeken niet alleen in het buitengebied hoe we een aantal smalle drukke wegen in Soest die wandelaars en fietsers nu noodgedwongen moeten delen met snelverkeer, veiliger en prettiger voor hen kunnen maken.

277253.explanation: Verkeerslichten worden zo afgesteld dat ze sneller groen licht geven aan wandelaars, fietsers en openbaar vervoer. Verkeerslichten worden voorzien van een timer en een regensensor.

277254.explanation: De straat wordt zo verdeeld dat veiligheid, ontmoeting en leefkwaliteit voor mens en dier voorop staan, niet de auto. Er moeten dus geen parkeerplaatsen bijkomen maar fietsenstallingen, bloemrijke bermen en kinderspeelplekken. Zo maken we ruimte voor schone lucht en een mooie omgeving.

277255.explanation: 'We kijken naar elkaar om in de samenleving. Daarom is het belangrijk dat de inzet van mantelzorgers wordt gezien, gewaardeerd en ondersteund. Er komt extra geld voor het tegengaan van overbelasting door het bieden van meer praktische ondersteuning om alle ballen in de lucht te kunnen houden.

277256.explanation: Een dorpspas kan sociale uitsluiting door armoede helpen tegengaan. De Partij voor de Dieren wil ook dat het openbaar vervoer gratis wordt voor mensen met een laag inkomen. Dit zou wat ons betreft ook gekoppeld kunnen worden aan de dorpspas.

277257.explanation: Eens, en daarnaast komt de gemeente met een plan om te voorkomen dat armoede wordt doorgegeven van generatie op generatie.

277258.explanation: De gemeente investeert in jongerencentra, buurthuizen, wijkcentra en ontmoetingsplekken, met daarbij ook speciale aandacht voor jongeren, ouderen en mensen met een beperking.

277259.explanation: Er komen meer doorfietsroutes om zo het fietsen te stimuleren als aantrekkelijk alternatief voor de auto. Nieuwe snelfietspaden gaan niet ten koste van groen en veiligheid van mens en dier.

277260.explanation: De gemeente past het evenementenplan aan, waarbij de hoofdstukken over natuurbescherming en dierenwelzijn uitgebreider en strenger opgesteld zullen worden. Wanneer het gaat om evenementen die geen negatieve effecten op dier, natuur en milieu hebben zijn soepelere regels bespreekbaar.

277261.explanation: Soest versterkt lokale initiatieven en de vorming van inwonersgroeperingen (zoals wijk/dorpsraden en buurtverenigingen).

277262.explanation: Er is een grote kans dat de windmolens die Amersfoort wil plaatsen hier ook komen, clusteren zorgt voor minder schade en overlast. De Partij voor de Dieren wil dat er bovenwettelijke maatregelen genomen zullen worden om de natuur te beschermen.

277263.explanation: De Partij voor de Dieren is tegen gedwongen fusies van welzijnsorganisaties. Welzijn draait om kwaliteit en betrokkenheid, niet om schaalvergroting.

277264.explanation: Er wordt ingezet op kleinschalige opvang, verspreid over de gemeente. Er moet niet zonder meer een asielzoekerscentrum in Soesterberg komen maar indien er een geschikte locatie is om een kleine groep mensen op te vangen kan het tot de mogelijkheden behoren.

277265.explanation: Eens, indien deze hulp gewenst is door de sekswerkers. Het beleid wordt mét in plaats van over sekswerkers gemaakt en hun stem wordt leidend.

277267.explanation: Vluchtelingen laten we zo snel mogelijk meedoen in onze gemeente. Soest helpt vluchtelingen bij het vinden van huisvesting, een gepaste baan en/of opleiding. Dit helpt bij een goede integratie en een gevoel iets bij te kunnen dragen.

277268.explanation: De gemeente koopt zelf grond om te bepalen wat erop wordt gebouwd. Zo kunnen er meer betaalbare woningen komen, omdat de gemeente geen winst hoeft te maken op de grond. Op bouwgrond van marktpartijen komen regels om te voorkomen dat grond wordt opgekocht en doorverkocht voor winst.

277213.explanation: Belastingen moeten in de ogen van Volt verantwoord, eerlijk en doelgericht zijn. De hoogte van de OZB hangt af van de financiële situatie en ambities van de gemeente. Het verhogen van de OZB kan vanuit solidariteit. De hoogte van de OZB is niet afhankelijk van eigen belang.

277214.explanation: Alle mensen zijn gelijkwaardig en hebben recht op menswaardige huisvesting. Als het nodig is om de doorstroom vanuit AZC's te bevorderen, krijgen vluchtelingen met een verblijfsvergunning voorrang. Indien de noodzaak er niet is, kan de voorrang beperkt worden.

277215.explanation: In het belang van de volksgezondheid en rekening houdend met inwoners met longproblemen, zal er een verbod moeten komen. Indien het handhaafbaar is, zou er bij bepaalde weersomstandigheden en in geval van langdurige stroomuitval een uitzondering gemaakt kunnen worden.

277216.explanation: Volt Soest gaat voor een inclusieve samenleving. In die samenleving is ook bijzondere aandacht voor ondersteuning van mensen die op dit moment niet (volledig) kunnen meedoen. Zo krijgen kinderen de kans om hun talent te ontdekken. Het meedoen kan bijvoorbeeld middels een dorpspas.

277217.explanation: Soest groeit en kent stadse problematieken als het gaat om openbare orde en veiligheid. In het kader van voorkomen en bestrijden van ondermijning en voor meer toezicht in het buitengebied, kunnen meer boa's helpen. Volt is alleen voor meer boa’s bij inzet binnen een integraal veiligheidsplan.

277218.explanation: In principe is één coffeeshop in Soest voldoende. Indien onderzoek uitwijst dat een tweede nodig is en bewoners aangeven dat ze behoefte hebben aan meer aanbod, dan kan overwogen worden om een tweede toe te staan. Mits dat kan op de juiste plek en zonder overlast te veroorzaken.

277219.explanation: Accessibility of the police is important. It is crucial for a neighborhood-oriented approach to policing. This benefits both the residents of the neighborhood and the police in keeping a feel for what is happening in the area.

277220.explanation: Unfortunately, this measure only drives up prices. We want more affordable housing, especially for single-person households, and the Affordable Rent Act must be enforced more strictly. It should also be easier to split large homes.

277221.explanation: The demand for social rental housing in Soest is high. We prioritize housing seekers from Soest (and Soesterberg). However, the solution lies in building more homes, enforcing the owner-occupancy obligation, and splitting large homes. This will create space for people who work in Soest.

277222.explanation: Given the need for social rental housing, raising the percentage to 40% makes sense. It must be prevented that entire neighborhoods consist solely of social rental housing, as this is not good for the neighborhood's balance.

277223.explanation: Where appropriate within the natural and urban landscape, we do not exclude the possibility of flats with six or more floors. Existing flats could also be topped with additional floors.

277224.explanation: Housing is a right for everyone. By converting existing offices, new housing can be created quickly. Subsidies for this reduce the profit gap compared to new builds, leading to faster construction of more homes. It also contributes to our transformation to a circular economy.

277228.explanation: Reducing speed increases traffic safety and lowers levels of fine dust and other nuisances. An exception can be made for main roads.

277229.explanation: Volt Soest is committed to effective participation; decisions are made together based on involvement. We use the expertise of residents and entrepreneurs. Through a careful participation process, administrative decisions improve in quality.

277231.explanation: It is good to appoint district aldermen. This also applies to other neighborhoods in Soest.

277232.explanation: Especially with today's challenges, there is a need for governance that listens well, seeks connection, and makes choices together with Soesters that suit them. Transparent governance is essential for trust in government.

277233.explanation: These locations are often cheaper for retailers, which causes traditional shopping areas to empty out. And Soest already has many different shopping areas.

277234.explanation: Volt Soest strives for a sustainable society. Housing and transport are developed sustainably, with attention to nature and the environment. In terms of sustainable entrepreneurship, the municipality supports entrepreneurs who want to jointly generate sustainable energy.

277236.explanation: Extra bureaucracy and residual waste are the end stage of consumption. Overconsumption must be tackled at the source. Moreover, it tempts people to dump waste instead of properly disposing of it.

277238.explanation: The question is whether this is a municipal task. It also depends on the situation. It must be good for the farmer but also good for the environment.

277239.explanation: Parks, playgrounds, and green spaces help residents maintain mental health at any age. We work on green oases within inhabited areas so every resident can reach greenery within 10 minutes on foot.

277241.explanation: We invest in (local) art and culture because a society without art and culture is a dead society. Art and culture foster connection and development. How this happens is up to the residents. If a permanent bandstand adds value, it should be realized.

277242.explanation: Children who play in green spaces are healthier, both physically and mentally. Europe provides subsidies for this, which we want to utilize.

277243.explanation: Sports and exercise are crucial for a healthy society.

277245.explanation: Where it enhances residents' safety, street lighting should be used more intelligently rather than more extensively. However, a broader view is necessary. Vegetation and buildings can also create unsafe situations. We work together with residents to determine what needs to change.

277247.explanation: If a long-term vision shows it is necessary to build a residential area behind De Schans, then we will do so—but in a sustainable, (environmentally) inclusive, and green manner. Together with the residents and entrepreneurs of Soest, we will decide in advance how many social rental homes need to be built.

277248.explanation: Buildings categorized as social real estate should be acquired by the municipality itself. It is advisable that such properties can be used in multiple ways, so multifunctional use should be promoted.

277249.explanation: Construction must be sustainable and profitable. However, the preference is for regular housing construction as soon as possible.

277250.explanation: There are already not enough parking spaces on the street; even Oude Marktplein is regularly full. Closing it off would reduce parking spots further. Additionally, it limits parking options close by for those with mobility issues.

277251.explanation: Instead of a mandatory counter-performance, Volt prefers support toward employment with language and training programs. Whether someone should receive less money depends on their personal situation, but financial worries cause enormous stress. Tailored solutions are needed here.

277252.explanation: Residents of Soest must be able to move safely on the streets anywhere and at any time, whether walking, cycling, or driving. Separate bike paths are safer for traffic.

277253.explanation: Our residents have the right to a safe and livable future. A future with clean air, clean water, space for nature, and affordable clean energy. Therefore, bicycle transportation must be encouraged as much as possible.

277254.explanation: We aim for fewer cars in residential neighborhoods and well-accessible shopping centers. Where necessary, we provide more parking spaces.

277255.explanation: Volt wants to continuously support informal caregivers and volunteers financially—for example, to follow courses or arrange replacement when going on vacation.

277256.explanation: Volt Soest advocates for an inclusive society. In such a society, special attention is given to supporting people who currently cannot fully participate. These Soesters receive a village pass, which offers discounts on activities in the municipality of Soest.

277257.explanation: Preventing financial problems contributes to residents' well-being. But ending up in debt can happen to anyone. Volt advocates a pause button, so those who get into debt can take a moment, experience less stress, and avoid deeper debt.

277258.explanation: Every neighborhood should have a community center. Activities that promote social cohesion in the neighborhood are organized there. This can also relate to building resilient communities.

277259.explanation: Volt supports clean energy and clean mobility. This also leads to a cleaner living environment. Fast cycling routes are a sustainable investment that furthermore results in a cleaner environment.

277260.explanation: Events have cultural and economic value, but there is also a need to limit nuisance. We seek a middle ground where both organizers and residents are heard. Rules for small events are applied based on common sense.

277261.explanation: Neighborhood maintenance teams will be reinstated to make plans to improve their neighborhood, especially to enhance social cohesion. Neighborhood budgets can be jointly requested and spent by residents.

277262.explanation: First, clarity must be established whether placing wind turbines is necessary. Then the best location for them should be determined.

277263.explanation: Every welfare organization has its own approach and specialties. They complement each other. However, there should be attention to effective and efficient deployment of welfare organizations. Welfare organizations must have a fixed point of contact within the municipality.

277264.explanation: The municipality of Soest must comply with the Distribution Act. This means that an asylum seekers' center (AZC) will have to be established in Soest. With an AZC at Kamp van Zeist, it is difficult to justify an additional AZC in Soesterberg.

277265.explanation: Volt views sex work as an equal part of the service sector and wants to eliminate the stigma on sex workers. Where desired and if sex workers themselves want, the municipality can guide them toward other work.

277267.explanation: In Soest, everyone participates. Where possible, the municipality can help guide status holders to work—but based on the same rights and obligations that apply to other Soest residents. Status holders greatly benefit from meaningful work, which also prevents boredom and related behavior.

277268.explanation: The principle is that the municipality is not a land trader. However, situations may arise where the municipality can purchase land to build houses—not to speculate with land but to enable affordable and social housing.

277213.explanation: We first check if there is structurally leftover money and use that better. Then we work smarter to reduce costs without compromising our plans or quality. We also believe it is fair that those who make use pay more through fees. An increase in property tax (OZB) is the last resort.

277214.explanation: We must comply with the Housing Act 2014, which states that we are obliged to house status holders.

277215.explanation: The use of open fireplaces and wood stoves is nationally permitted. At the same time, wood burning can locally cause nuisance and health problems. Therefore, we choose neither unconditional allowance (agree) nor a general ban (disagree). Our focus is on clear instructions and good information to limit nuisance and health risks. That is why neither option fits our position best.

277216.explanation: Sport and exercise must be accessible to everyone; money should not be a barrier.

277217.explanation: Safe neighborhoods require visibility and enforcement. Focusing on safety/enforcement and cooperation within the neighborhood supports more (or sufficient) public order officer capacity.

277218.explanation: The Christian Union wants to combat harm caused by drugs and undermining activities.

277219.explanation: A weekly consultation hour is a good arrangement, provided capacity allows it.

277220.explanation: The housing market is tight for starters. Support can help.

277221.explanation: We adhere to regional agreements on housing allocation. This ensures a fair distribution of social rental homes and prevents municipalities from competing against each other or passing on problems.

277222.explanation: Nationally and locally it has been agreed that about 30% of new construction consists of social rental housing. This allows for a balanced mix of social rental, mid-level rental, and affordable owner-occupied homes, suited to the local housing task.

277223.explanation: We are not fundamentally in favor of more high-rise buildings, but do not rule it out either. The height of buildings should be weighed per location, fitting the environment and the village character. This is not a yes position but a weighing position.

277224.explanation: By converting vacant offices into homes, we can build faster without using extra space. The municipality can support this by speeding up procedures and, where necessary, providing financial help, especially when it involves affordable housing.

277228.explanation: Within the built-up area, a maximum speed limit of 30 km/h applies. On major public transport and connecting roads, 50 km/h remains permitted.

277229.explanation: A citizens' assembly can help with important choices, as long as it has an advisory role, everyone can participate, and the municipal council and executive clearly show what they do with the advice.

277231.explanation: Soesterberg deserves fixed administrative attention. A dedicated contact alderman ensures a clear point of contact and good connection with the village and involved partners.

277232.explanation: Transparency and integrity are important. A public overview of consultations with interest groups increases trust, taking into account privacy and security where needed.

277233.explanation: More shops outside existing shopping areas can weaken the center. We prefer to invest in strong, vital shopping districts and allow exceptions only if they truly strengthen the center.

277234.explanation: Generating sustainable energy together fits the energy transition and local ownership. The municipality can support this by facilitating and, where needed, offering guarantees or loans, and by seeking solutions for the full electricity grid.

277236.explanation: Paying per amount of residual waste can help improve waste separation. We only want to introduce this if it practically works well for Soest and does not have unwanted side effects, such as more littering.

277238.explanation: Supporting farmers in nature and landscape management can in some cases contribute to biodiversity and landscape. We do not see this as a standard measure or automatic subsidy, but as tailor-made. Only where it fits the local situation and is agreed upon with farmers can this be an appropriate instrument. Therefore, we choose neither.

277239.explanation: Good maintenance of parks, playgrounds, and greenery ensures a pleasant and safe living environment. We especially want to invest extra where there is backlog, with attention to quality and biodiversity.

277241.explanation: A permanent bandstand can stimulate meeting and culture in an accessible way. This works well if management is clear, programming is done together with clubs, and there is attention for noise and nuisance.

277242.explanation: Green schoolyards contribute to more nature and biodiversity and provide a healthy living environment for children.

277243.explanation: New neighborhoods must have space for outdoor sports, such as exercise spots, routes, and simple fitness facilities in public spaces.

277245.explanation: We want to limit light pollution and therefore opt for smart lighting, such as dimming or using sensors, instead of adding more street lighting everywhere.

277247.explanation: The polder behind De Schans is a logical location for housing. Here we can build homes that match Soest's needs, with attention to affordability, quality, and good integration into the surroundings.

277248.explanation: Meeting places are important, but the municipality does not always need to buy buildings itself. We first look at cooperation, shared use, and support. Only if that does not work and it is really necessary can purchasing be an option.

277249.explanation: Temporary housing can help quickly relieve housing pressure. That is only possible if the homes are of good quality, management is well organized, and the location will later actually be used for housing construction.

277250.explanation: A car-free square fits a livable environment and gives space to walking and cycling. This is possible as long as emergency services and suppliers can access it well and there is an alternative for parking.

277251.explanation: We choose the human scale and tailor-made solutions. We help people with guidance and removing barriers. We do not automatically reduce benefits but assess the needs per situation, with guidance as the starting point.

277252.explanation: Where needed, we opt for separated cycle paths. In rural areas, separating cyclists and cars increases safety and makes cycling more attractive.

277253.explanation: Cyclists and pedestrians deserve priority for safe and smooth flow. This fits traffic safety and the STOMP approach. With the app 'Da’s zo gefietst' this can already be improved.

277254.explanation: More parking spaces as a general solution costs greenery and space and leads to more car use. We first choose smarter parking, shared mobility, and good cycling facilities. Only with clear bottlenecks is targeted expansion needed.

277255.explanation: Informal carers deserve good support. Extra efforts, such as guidance and a carer’s break, help sustain care and prevent overload.

277256.explanation: A village pass helps remove barriers so that everyone can participate in activities in Soest. It is important that this pass works simply and is granted automatically where possible.

277257.explanation: We focus on preventing debts and early detection of money problems, especially among young people. Good information about money and debts is naturally part of this.

277258.explanation: Meeting places are important for social cohesion and safety. Where they are lacking and needed, we want to ensure that new meeting places are created.

277259.explanation: We continue to invest in fast and safe cycling routes, together with the province and the region.

277260.explanation: We want to keep rules as simple as possible and give space to initiatives from residents. Therefore, rules for small events can be relaxed, for example with a notification requirement, clear standard conditions, and one point of contact.

277261.explanation: Neighborhoods get more space to make their own plans and, where possible, their own budget. Residents can also take over tasks from the municipality if they think they can do it better.

277262.explanation: We support sustainable energy but do not want to predetermine where wind turbines will be located. Careful research and good involvement of local residents are needed first before choosing a location.

277263.explanation: Collaboration between organizations is important, but a mandatory merger is not necessary. We prefer good agreements, a unified direction, and cooperation per neighborhood, so organizations remain flexible and close to residents.

277264.explanation: Because there is already a large reception center on the border of Zeist and Soesterberg, an additional large site in Soesterberg is not desirable. Therefore, we choose to spread out and have small-scale reception elsewhere in the municipality, with good supervision and clear agreements.

277265.explanation: We want to support sex workers who want to stop, for example by helping them find other work. This fits with the approach against human trafficking and exploitation.

277267.explanation: We want to help status holders find work faster by practically organizing language learning, integration, and employment and by supporting employers in this process.

277268.explanation: We choose active land policy so that the municipality maintains control over the pace, quality, and affordability of housing development.

277216.explanation: Associations are an important social factor, but we want fair subsidy schemes. This proposal would mean that children of parents who are above the minimum income threshold but cannot afford the membership fee would fall through the cracks.

277229.explanation: We want a binding referendum for all residents. Citizens' assemblies are often directed by civil servants, and the participants often do not represent a good reflection of the population.

277247.explanation: We see arguments for and against this development. We want to present this choice to residents before proceeding with construction.

statements.title: In the municipality of Soest the use of open fireplaces and wood stoves should remain permitted.

titleParts.text: In de gemeente Soest moet het gebruik van open haarden en

titleParts.glossary: Wood-burning stoves used to heat a home.

statements.title: Of the new homes to be built in the municipality of Soest, 40% should be social rental housing. Currently about 23% of all homes are social rentals.

titleParts.text: Van de nieuw te bouwen woningen in de gemeente Soest moet 40% een

titleParts.glossary: A rental home with a low rent (in 2026 at most €932.93 per month) for people with a low income.

titleParts.text: zijn. Nu is ongeveer 23% van alle woningen sociale huur.

statements.title: The municipality should provide schools with funding for more plants and trees on their schoolyards.

statements.theme: Priority for people with refugee status
statements.title: The municipality should not give refugees with a residence permit (status holders) priority for social rental housing.

statements.title: All welfare organizations in the municipality should merge into one organisation.
Traffic lights for cyclists should turn green more quickly.

shootoutStatements.title: The municipality should spend more money on maintenance of parks, playgrounds and public green spaces.
When allocating social housing, applicants from the municipality should be given priority.
The maximum speed limit in built-up areas should be set to 30 km/h.
The municipality should allow more shops outside the existing shopping areas.
Vacant offices should be converted into housing with subsidies from the municipality.
In new residential neighborhoods, the municipality should set up places where residents can exercise outdoors for free.
Entrepreneurs who want to jointly generate sustainable energy should receive financial support from the municipality.
There must not be an asylum seekers' centre in Soesterberg.
The municipality should reserve money per neighborhood for residents' plans. The residents of the neighborhood choose which plan is implemented.
The municipality should continue to pay sports club memberships for children whose parents or caregivers have little money.
The municipality should ask the police to hold a weekly office hour with the neighborhood police officer.
The municipality should continue to provide information about financial problems.
The municipality should relax the rules for small events.
The property tax (ozb) should be increased.
The municipality should do more to help status holders find paid employment.
The municipality should spend more money on supporting informal caregivers.
